<skill_content name="customize-opencode">
# Skill: customize-opencode

<!--
  Built-in skill. Name and description are registered in code at
  packages/opencode/src/skill/index.ts (see CUSTOMIZE_OPENCODE_SKILL_NAME
  and CUSTOMIZE_OPENCODE_SKILL_DESCRIPTION). The body below becomes the
  skill's content.
-->

# Customizing opencode

opencode validates its own config strictly and refuses to start when a field
is wrong. The shapes below cover the common surface area, but they are a
**summary, not the source of truth**.

## Full schema reference

The authoritative list of every config option — with field types, enums,
defaults, and descriptions — lives in the published JSON Schema:

**<https://opencode.ai/config.json>**

If a field is not documented in this skill, or you need to confirm an exact
shape before writing config, **fetch that URL and read the schema directly**
rather than guessing. opencode hard-fails on invalid config, so the cost of a
wrong shape is a broken startup.

Independently, every `opencode.json` should declare
`"$schema": "https://opencode.ai/config.json"` so the user's editor catches
mistakes as they type.

## Applying changes

Config is loaded once when opencode starts and is not hot-reloaded. After
saving changes to `opencode.json`, an agent file, a skill, a plugin, or any
other config-time file, **tell the user to quit and restart opencode** for
the changes to take effect. The running session will keep using the
already-loaded config until then.

## Where files live

| Scope                         | Path                                                                                                                      |
| ----------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Project config                | `./opencode.json`, `./opencode.jsonc`, or `.opencode/opencode.json` (opencode walks up from the cwd to the worktree root) |
| Global config                 | `~/.config/opencode/opencode.json` (NOT `~/.opencode/`)                                                                   |
| Project agents                | `.opencode/agent/<name>.md` or `.opencode/agents/<name>.md`                                                               |
| Global agents                 | `~/.config/opencode/agent(s)/<name>.md`                                                                                   |
| Project skills                | `.opencode/skill(s)/<name>/SKILL.md`                                                                                      |
| Global skills                 | `~/.config/opencode/skill(s)/<name>/SKILL.md`                                                                             |
| External skills (auto-loaded) | `~/.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md`, `~/.agents/skills/<name>/SKILL.md`                                                    |

Configs from each scope are deep-merged. Project overrides global. Unknown
top-level keys in `opencode.json` are rejected with `ConfigInvalidError`.

## opencode.json

Every field is optional.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://opencode.ai/config.json",
  "username": "string",
  "model": "provider/model-id",
  "small_model": "provider/model-id",
  "default_agent": "agent-name",
  "shell": "/bin/zsh",
  "logLevel": "DEBUG" | "INFO" | "WARN" | "ERROR",
  "share": "manual" | "auto" | "disabled",
  "autoupdate": true | false | "notify",
  "snapshot": true,
  "instructions": ["AGENTS.md", "docs/style.md"],

  "skills": {
    "paths": [".opencode/skills", "/abs/path/to/skills"],
    "urls": ["https://example.com/.well-known/skills/"]
  },

  "agent": {
    "my-agent": {
      "model": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6",
      "mode": "subagent",
      "description": "...",
      "permission": { "edit": "deny" }
    }
  },

  "command": {
    "deploy": { "description": "...", "prompt": "..." }
  },

  "provider": {
    "anthropic": { "options": { "apiKey": "..." } }
  },
  "disabled_providers": ["openai"],
  "enabled_providers": ["anthropic"],

  "mcp": {
    "playwright": {
      "type": "local",
      "command": ["npx", "-y", "@playwright/mcp"],
      "enabled": true,
      "env": {}
    },
    "remote-thing": {
      "type": "remote",
      "url": "https://...",
      "headers": { "Authorization": "Bearer ..." }
    }
  },

  "plugin": [
    "opencode-gemini-auth",
    "opencode-foo@1.2.3",
    "./local-plugin.ts",
    ["opencode-bar", { "option": "value" }]
  ],

  "permission": {
    "edit": "deny",
    "bash": { "git *": "allow", "*": "ask" }
  },

  "formatter": false,
  "lsp": false,

  "experimental": {
    "primary_tools": ["edit"],
    "mcp_timeout": 30000
  },

  "tool_output": { "max_lines": 200, "max_bytes": 8192 },

  "compaction": { "auto": true, "tail_turns": 15 }
}
```

Shape notes worth being explicit about:

- `model` always carries a provider prefix: `"anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6"`.
- `skills` is an object with `paths` and/or `urls`, not an array.
- `agent` is an object keyed by agent name, not an array.
- `plugin` is an array of strings or `[name, options]` tuples, not an object.
- `mcp[name].command` is an array of strings, never a single string. `type` is required.
- `permission` is either a string action or an object keyed by tool name.

## Skills

opencode's skill loader scans for `**/SKILL.md` inside skill directories. The
file is named `SKILL.md` exactly, and lives in its own folder named after the
skill:

```
.opencode/skills/my-skill/SKILL.md
```

Frontmatter:

```markdown
---
name: my-skill
description: One sentence covering what this skill does AND when to trigger it. Front-load the literal keywords or filenames the user is likely to say.
---

# My Skill

(skill body in markdown: instructions, examples, references)
```

- `name` is required, lowercase hyphen-separated, up to 64 chars, and matches the folder name.
- `description` is effectively required: skills without one are filtered out and never surfaced to the model. Cover both _what_ the skill does and _when_ to use it. Write in third person ("Use when...", not "I help with..."). Front-load concrete trigger keywords and filenames; gate with "Use ONLY when..." if the skill should stay quiet on adjacent topics.
- Optional: `license`, `compatibility`, `metadata` (string-string map).

Register skills from non-default locations via `skills.paths` (scanned
recursively for `**/SKILL.md`) and `skills.urls` (each URL serves a list of
skills).

## Agents

Two ways to define an agent. Use the file form for anything non-trivial.

### Inline (in `opencode.json`)

```json
{
  "agent": {
    "my-reviewer": {
      "description": "Reviews PRs for style violations.",
      "mode": "subagent",
      "model": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6",
      "permission": { "edit": "deny", "bash": "ask" },
      "prompt": "You are a strict PR reviewer..."
    }
  }
}
```

### File

```

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

PORT="${OPENCODE_PORT:-4096}"
STATE_DIR="${XDG_STATE_HOME:-$HOME/.local/state}/opencode"
PASSWORD_FILE="$STATE_DIR/server.password"

mkdir -p "$STATE_DIR"

if [ -z "${OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD:-}" ]; then
	if [ ! -s "$PASSWORD_FILE" ]; then
		umask 077
		if command -v openssl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
			openssl rand -base64 32 >"$PASSWORD_FILE"
		else
			uuidgen >"$PASSWORD_FILE"
		fi
	fi
	OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD="$(tr -d '\n' <"$PASSWORD_FILE")"
	export OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD

metadata.preview:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# OpenTUI probes Kitty graphics support by default. In tmux panes that reply can
# occasionally leak into Fish as `Gi=31337;OK`; keep image probing off for
# OpenCode while leaving terminal graphics enabled for other apps.
export OPENTUI_GRAPHICS="${OPENTUI_GRAPHICS:-0}"

for ocv_bin in /opt/homebrew/bin/ocv /usr/local/bin/ocv "$HOME/.local/bin/ocv" "$HOME/.bun/bin/ocv"; do
	if [ -x "$ocv_bin" ]; then
		exec "$ocv_bin" "$@"
	fi
done

if ! command -v ocv >/dev/null 2>&1; then
	printf 'opencode shim: ocv is not installed. Run: brew install leohenon/tap/ocv\n' >&2
	exit 127
fi

exec ocv "$@"

1: // OpenCode plugin: expose session metadata to shell commands.
2: // Tmux window colors are pane-local and owned by scripts/opencode/tmux-open.sh.
3: import type { Plugin } from "@opencode-ai/plugin"
4: 
5: export const SessionEnvPlugin: Plugin = async () => {
6:   let currentSessionID: string | null = null
7:   let currentModel: string | null = null
8: 
9:   return {
10:     event: async ({ event }) => {
11:       switch (event.type) {
12:         case "session.created": {
13:           const session = (event as any).properties?.info
14:           if (!session?.id) break
15:           currentSessionID = session.id
16:           break
17:         }
18: 
19:         case "message.updated": {
20:           const msg = (event as any).properties?.info
21:           if (msg?.role === "assistant" && msg?.modelID) {
22:             currentModel = msg.modelID
23:           }
24:           break
25:         }
26: 
27:         case "session.deleted": {
28:           currentSessionID = null
29:           currentModel = null
30:           break
31:         }
32: 
33:         case "server.instance.disposed": {
34:           // OpenCode can dispose transient server instances while the attached
35:           // TUI is still alive. Session metadata is cleared only on deletion.
36:           break
37:         }
38:       }
39:     },
40:     "shell.env": async (input, output) => {
41:       const sessionID = input.sessionID ?? currentSessionID
42:       if (sessionID) {
43:         currentSessionID = sessionID
44:         output.env.OPENCODE_SESSION_ID = sessionID
45:       }
46:       if (currentModel) {

1: // OpenCode plugin: capture SSE events for Entire + Diffview integration
2: // Writes a JSONL log for all events and persists diff snapshots so
3: // Diffview (inside Neovim) can replay or inspect AI edits later.
4: // Also mirrors summarized payloads into Entire hooks for checkpoints.
5: import type { Plugin } from "@opencode-ai/plugin"
6: import path from "node:path"
7: import { mkdir, appendFile, writeFile } from "node:fs/promises"
8: import { spawn } from "node:child_process"
9: import crypto from "node:crypto"
10: 
11: type OpencodeEvent = {
12:   type?: string
13:   timestamp?: string
14:   properties?: Record<string, any>
15: }
16: 
17: type NormalizedEvent = {
18:   type: string
19:   timestamp: string
20:   session_id: string | null
21:   message_id: string | null
22:   model: string | null
23:   summary: Record<string, unknown>
24: }
25: 
26: const DIFF_KEYS = [
27:   "patch",
28:   "diff",
29:   "content",
30:   "text",
31:   "body",
32:   "hunks",
33: ]
34: 
35: const DEFAULT_RECORDED_PREFIXES = [
36:   "session.",
37:   "tool.",
38:   "permission.",
39:   "message.patch.",
40:   "file.",
41: ]
42: 
43: const DEFAULT_RECORDED_EVENTS = new Set([
44:   "message.updated",
45:   "server.instance.disposed",
46:   "tui.toast.show",
47: ])
48: 
49: function looksLikeDiff(candidate: unknown): candidate is string {
50:   if (typeof candidate !== "string") return false
51:   const trimmed = candidate.trimStart()
52:   if (!trimmed) return false
53:   return (
54:     trimmed.startsWith("diff --git") ||
55:     trimmed.startsWith("@@") ||
56:     trimmed.startsWith("--- ") ||
57:     trimmed.includes("\n@@")
58:   )
59: }
60: 
61: function searchForDiff(value: unknown): string | null {
62:   if (!value) return null
63:   if (looksLikeDiff(value)) return value
64:   if (Array.isArray(value)) {
65:     for (const entry of value) {
66:       const diff = searchForDiff(entry)
67:       if (diff) return diff
68:     }
69:     return null
70:   }
71:   if (typeof value === "object") {
72:     for (const key of Object.keys(value as Record<string, unknown>)) {
73:       if (!DIFF_KEYS.includes(key) && typeof (value as any)[key] !== "object") {
74:         continue
75:       }
76:       const diff = searchForDiff((value as any)[key])
77:       if (diff) return diff
78:     }
79:   }
80:   return null
81: }
82: 
83: function sanitizeProperties(props: Record<string, unknown> | undefined) {
84:   if (!props) return {}
85:   const clone = JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(props))
86:   const diff = searchForDiff(clone)
87:   if (diff && diff.length > 2048) {
88:     ;(clone as any).diff_preview = `${diff.slice(0, 1024)}…`
89:   }
90:   return clone
91: }
92: 
93: function extractTarget(props: Record<string, unknown> | undefined): string | null {
94:   if (!props) return null
95:   const candidates = [
96:     props.path,
97:     props.file,
98:     props.filepath,
99:     props.target,
100:     props.targetPath,
101:     props.location,
102:     props?.info?.path,
103:     props?.info?.file,
104:   ]
105:   for (const candidate of candidates) {
106:     if (typeof candidate === "string" && candidate.trim().length > 0) {
107:       return candidate
108:     }
109:   }
110:   return null
111: }
112: 
113: function slugify(value: string | null) {
114:   if (!value) return "workspace"
115:   return value
116:     .toLowerCase()
117:     .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, "-")
118:     .replace(/^-+|-+$/g, "")
119:     .slice(0, 40) || "workspace"
120: }
121: 
122: async function writeJsonl(filePath: string, payload: NormalizedEvent) {
123:   await appendFile(filePath, `${JSON.stringify(payload)}\n`, "utf8")
124: }
125: 
126: function callEntireHook(hook: string, payload: Record<string, unknown>) {
127:   if (process.env.OPENCODE_SSE_DISABLE_ENTIRE === "1" || process.env.OPENCODE_SSE_ENTIRE !== "1") {
128:     return Promise.resolve()

138:       return
139:     }
140:     child.on("error", () => resolve())
141:     if (!child.stdin) {

146:     child.stdin.end()

148:   })
149: }

152:   if (process.env.OPENCODE_SSE_FULL === "1") return true
153:   if (DEFAULT_RECORDED_EVENTS.has(type)) return true
154:   return DEFAULT_RECORDED_PREFIXES.some((prefix) => type.startsWith(prefix))
155: }

157: function normalizeEvent(event: OpencodeEvent): NormalizedEvent {
158:   const props = event.properties ?? {}

1: // Entire CLI plugin for OpenCode
2: // Auto-generated by `entire enable --agent opencode`
3: // Do not edit manually — changes will be overwritten on next install.
4: // Requires Bun runtime (used by OpenCode's plugin system for loading ESM plugins).

6: 
7: export const EntirePlugin: Plugin = async ({ directory }) => {
8:   const ENTIRE_CMD = 'entire'
9:   // Track seen user messages to fire turn-start only once per message
10:   const seenUserMessages = new Set<string>()
11:   // Track current session ID for message events (which don't include sessionID)
12:   let currentSessionID: string | null = null
13:   // Track the model used by the most recent assistant message
14:   let currentModel: string | null = null

16:   const messageStore = new Map<string, any>()
17: 
18:   /**
19:    * Build the shell command for a hook invocation.
20:    * Uses sh -c so that shell command substitution in ENTIRE_CMD
21:    * (e.g., $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) for local-dev) is interpreted.
22:    */
23:   function hookCmd(hookName: string): string[] {
24:     return ["sh", "-c", `${ENTIRE_CMD} hooks opencode ${hookName}`]
25:   }
26: 
27:   /**
28:    * Pipe JSON payload to an entire hooks command (async).
29:    * Errors are logged but never thrown — plugin failures must not crash OpenCode.
30:    */
31:   async function callHook(hookName: string, payload: Record<string, unknown>) {
32:     try {
33:       const json = JSON.stringify(payload)
34:       const proc = Bun.spawn(hookCmd(hookName), {
35:         cwd: directory,
36:         stdin: new Blob([json + "\n"]),
37:         stdout: "ignore",
38:         stderr: "ignore",
39:       })
40:       await proc.exited
41:     } catch {
42:       // Silently ignore — plugin failures must not crash OpenCode
43:     }
44:   }
45: 
46:   /**
47:    * Synchronous variant for hooks that fire near process exit (turn-end, session-end).
48:    * `opencode run` breaks its event loop on the same session.status idle event that
49:    * triggers turn-end. The async callHook would be killed before completing.
50:    * Bun.spawnSync blocks the event loop, preventing exit until the hook finishes.
51:    */
52:   function callHookSync(hookName: string, payload: Record<string, unknown>) {
53:     try {
54:       const json = JSON.stringify(payload)
55:       Bun.spawnSync(hookCmd(hookName), {
56:         cwd: directory,
57:         stdin: new TextEncoder().encode(json + "\n"),
58:         stdout: "ignore",
59:         stderr: "ignore",
60:       })
61:     } catch {
62:       // Silently ignore — plugin failures must not crash OpenCode
63:     }
64:   }
65: 
66:   return {
67:     event: async ({ event }) => {
68:       try {
69:         switch (event.type) {
70:           case "session.created": {
71:             const session = (event as any).properties?.info
72:             if (!session?.id) break
73:             // Reset per-session tracking state when switching sessions.
74:             if (currentSessionID !== session.id) {
75:               seenUserMessages.clear()
76:               messageStore.clear()
77:               currentModel = null
78:               await callHook("session-start", {
79:                 session_id: session.id,
80:               })
81:             }
82:             currentSessionID = session.id
83:             break
84:           }
85: 
86:           case "message.updated": {
87:             const msg = (event as any).properties?.info
88:             if (!msg) break
89:             // Store message metadata (role, time, tokens, etc.)
90:             messageStore.set(msg.id, msg)
91:             // Track model from assistant messages
92:             if (msg.role === "assistant" && msg.modelID) {
93:               currentModel = msg.modelID
94:             }
95:             break
96:           }
97: 
98:           case "message.part.updated": {
99:             const part = (event as any).properties?.part
100:             if (!part?.messageID) break
101: 
102:             // Fire turn-start on the first text part of a new user message
103:             const msg = messageStore.get(part.messageID)
104:             if (msg?.role === "user" && part.type === "text" && !seenUserMessages.has(msg.id)) {
105:               seenUserMessages.add(msg.id)
106:               const sessionID = msg.sessionID ?? currentSessionID
107:               if (sessionID) {
108:                 await callHook("turn-start", {
109:                   session_id: sessionID,
110:                   prompt: part.text ?? "",
111:                   model: currentModel ?? "",
112:                 })
113:               }
114:             }
115:             break
116:           }
117: 
118:           case "session.status": {
119:             // session.status fires in both TUI and non-interactive (run) mode.
120:             // session.idle is deprecated and not reliably emitted in run mode.
121:             const props = (event as any).properties
122:             if (props?.status?.type !== "idle") break
123:             const sessionID = props?.sessionID ?? currentSessionID
124:             if (!sessionID) break
125:             // Use sync variant: `opencode run` exits on the same idle event,
126:             // so an async hook would be killed before completing.
127:             callHookSync("turn-end", {
128:               session_id: sessionID,
129:               model: currentModel ?? "",
130:             })
131:             break
132:           }
133: 
134:           case "session.compacted": {
135:             const sessionID = (event as any).properties?.sessionID
136:             if (!sessionID) break
137:             await callHook("compaction", {
138:               session_id: sessionID,
139:             })
140:             break
141:           }
142: 
143:           case "session.deleted": {
144:             const session = (event as any).properties?.info
145:             if (!session?.id) break
146:             seenUserMessages.clear()
147:             messageStore.clear()
148:             currentSessionID = null
149:             // Use sync variant: session-end may fire during shutdown.
150:             callHookSync("session-end", {
151:               session_id: session.id,
152:             })
153:             break
154:           }
155: 
156:           case "server.instance.disposed": {
157:             // Fires when OpenCode shuts down (TUI close or `opencode run` exit).
158:             // session.deleted only fires on explicit user deletion, not on quit,
159:             // so this is the only reliable way to end sessions on exit.
160:             if (!currentSessionID) break
161:             const sessionID = currentSessionID
162:             seenUserMessages.clear()
163:             messageStore.clear()
164:             currentSessionID = null
165:             // Use sync variant: this is the last event before process exit.
166:             callHookSync("session-end", {
167:               session_id: sessionID,
168:             })
169:             break
170:           }
171:         }
172:       } catch {
173:         // Silently ignore — plugin failures must not crash OpenCode
174:       }
175:     },
176:   }
177: }

</content>

state.title: Shows launchd service state

parts.text:
**Inspecting DCP Configs**

I need to come up with a plan and avoid any edits. Perhaps I should check the open config for DCP and see what’s going on. There's a warning from the DCP plugin, so it's possible the user pasted some Consolee(Focused) info, which might relate to the DCP UI. It seems like the @tarquinen/opencode-dcp plugin likely has EventTarget listeners that I need to inspect further. Let's dive into those DCP configs!

There are four active `ocv attach` clients against one shared server. That matters because attach clients can add server-side event subscriptions; the warning threshold is 11 listeners, so it may be triggered by multiple clients plus plugins, not by shell inefficiency.

metadata.preview:
function oc --description "Attach OpenCode TUI to the shared local server"
    set -l port (set -q OPENCODE_PORT; and echo $OPENCODE_PORT; or echo 4096)
    set -l url "http://127.0.0.1:$port"
    set -l label com.dotfiles.opencode-serve
    set -l state_home (set -q XDG_STATE_HOME; and echo $XDG_STATE_HOME; or echo "$HOME/.local/state")
    set -l password_file "$state_home/opencode/server.password"

    if not set -q OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD; and test -s "$password_file"
        set -gx OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD (string collect <$password_file)
    end
    set -q OPENCODE_SERVER_USERNAME; or set -gx OPENCODE_SERVER_USERNAME opencode

    if command -q curl
        command curl -fsS -u "$OPENCODE_SERVER_USERNAME:$OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD" "$url/path" >/dev/null 2>/dev/null
        if test $status -ne 0; and command -q launchctl
            set -l uid (id -u)
            launchctl kickstart -k "gui/$uid/$label" >/dev/null 2>/dev/null; or opencode-service start >/dev/null 2>/dev/null
            for _ in 1 2 3 4 5
                command curl -fsS -u "$OPENCODE_SERVER_USERNAME:$OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD" "$url/path" >/dev/null 2>/dev/null
                and break

22: 	[ -f "$source_plist" ] || return 1
23: 	mkdir -p "$(dirname "$plist")"
24: 	local escaped_home escaped_dotfiles_root
25: 	escaped_home=$(printf '%s\n' "$HOME" | sed 's/[\/&]/\\&/g')
26: 	escaped_dotfiles_root=$(printf '%s\n' "$HOME/dotfiles" | sed 's/[\/&]/\\&/g')
27: 	sed \
28: 		-e "s/__HOME__/${escaped_home}/g" \
29: 		-e "s/__DOTFILES_ROOT__/${escaped_dotfiles_root}/g" \
30: 		"$source_plist" >"$plist"
31: }
32: 
33: start_server() {
34: 	command -v launchctl >/dev/null 2>&1 || return 0
35: 	local uid
36: 	uid="$(id -u)"
37: 	launchctl kickstart -k "gui/${uid}/${label}" >/dev/null 2>&1 && return 0
38: 	materialize_plist || return 0
39: 	launchctl bootstrap "gui/${uid}" "$plist" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
40: 	launchctl kickstart -k "gui/${uid}/${label}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
41: }
42: 
43: if ! check_server; then
44: 	start_server
45: 	for _ in 1 2 3 4 5; do
46: 		check_server && break
47: 		sleep 0.2
48: 	done
49: fi
50: 
51: exec opencode attach "${url}" --dir "${OPENCODE_DIR:-$PWD}" "$@"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

port="${OPENCODE_PORT:-4096}"
password_file="${XDG_STATE_HOME:-$HOME/.local/state}/opencode/server.password"
url="http://127.0.0.1:${port}"
label="com.dotfiles.opencode-serve"
plist="$HOME/Library/LaunchAgents/${label}.plist"
source_plist="$HOME/dotfiles/Library/LaunchAgents/${label}.plist"

if [ -z "${OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD:-}" ] && [ -s "$password_file" ]; then
	export OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD="$(tr -d '\n' <"$password_file")"
fi
export OPENCODE_SERVER_USERNAME="${OPENCODE_SERVER_USERNAME:-opencode}"

check_server() {
	command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1 || return 0
	curl -fsS -u REDACTED "${url}/path" >/dev/null 2>&1
}

parts.text:
**Evaluating event listeners**

I’m analyzing a situation with at least 11 event listeners for the server's event bus, which includes active clients and plugins, like local plugins (5, including DCP). It's possible that listeners are being added for destroyed events. Maybe this is coming from React/Ink screen in OpenTUI P2. I don't want to edit during plan mode, so I'll first test with one client and close stale connections or restart the service. If that resolves the warning, I might need to look further into the fanout issue. I'll also consider disabling DCP temporarily.

**Considering client implementation**

I'm thinking about how I shouldn't make any edits right now. If I decide to implement changes, I might add a command like `opencode-service clients` to display attached PIDs and consider that `opencode-service restart` should notify active clients. However, I don't think optional `OPENCODE_NODE_OPTIONS` is necessary. The shared persistent server does support multiple attachments intentionally, but I wonder if opencode/ocv or DCP can handle many clients reliably. It's something worth exploring further!

No, `scripts/opencode/serve.sh` is probably not the thing to optimise.

Findings:

- `serve.sh` only sets env/password and then `exec`s `ocv serve --port 4096`.
- Launchd shows the server has only run twice, not crash-looped.
- Live processes show one server plus four `ocv attach` clients.
- The warning is from Bun/Node EventTarget listener tracking inside bundled `effect` code, not from the shell wrapper.
- DCP is enabled via `@tarquinen/opencode-dcp@latest`, and the UI text you pasted looks consistent with DCP/chat notification activity, but the stack does not point directly to local plugin code.

Most likely causes:

1. Multiple long-lived `ocv attach` clients stacking event subscriptions on the shared server.
2. A plugin, likely DCP or the upstream `opencode-with-claude`/OpenCode runtime, adding listeners per event/session without cleanup.
3. Less likely: stale attach clients left behind after terminal/tmux pane exits.

Recommended plan:

1. Close stale `ocv attach` clients and restart the service once with `opencode-service restart`.
2. Reproduce with only one attached client.
3. If clean, the shared-server multi-attach model is triggering the listener threshold.
4. If it still happens, temporarily disable DCP and test again.
5. If confirmed, optimise the wrappers by adding visibility/cleanup for attach clients, not by changing `serve.sh`.

A useful small improvement would be adding an `opencode-service clients` command to list active `ocv attach` processes, and possibly a safe cleanup command for stale attaches. I would not hide the warning with `setMaxListeners` or `--no-warnings` unless we prove it is harmless.
How are you defining what a stale attach is?

A “stale attach” is an `ocv attach` client process that is still alive but no longer has a useful interactive owner.

I would not define it as “old”. Long-lived attach clients can be legitimate if they’re in active tmux panes.

Practical definition:

- Parent terminal/tmux pane is gone, but `ocv attach` is still running.
- Its TTY is missing or detached in a way that no user can interact with it.
- It has been idle for a long time and its working directory/session is not visible in any active tmux window.
- Multiple attaches exist for the same repo/session where only one pane is actually in use.
- The process survives after closing the terminal/pane that launched it.

In your current snapshot, I would call these “suspicious”, not proven stale:

```text
ocv attach ... --dir /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles          21h47m
ocv attach ... --dir /Users/shahe.islam/work/aws...       21h50m
ocv attach ... --dir /Users/shahe.islam/obsidian          18h43m
ocv attach ... --dir /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles          02m13s
```

The two `dotfiles` attaches are the most suspicious because they attach to the same shared server and same dir. But if both correspond to visible tmux panes, they are not stale.

A safe stale check would be:

1. List `ocv attach` PIDs, parent PIDs, elapsed time, TTY, and `--dir`.
2. Map each PID/PPID to tmux panes with `tmux list-panes -a -F`.
3. Mark as stale only when there is no matching live pane/TTY, or the pane command no longer matches the attach process.
4. Never kill based on age alone.

So my earlier “stale attach” wording means “orphaned/unreachable attach client”, not “old attach client”.

- Parent terminal/tmux pane is gone, but ocv attach is still 
running.
- Its TTY is missing or detached in a way that no user can 
interact with it. 

These should always automatically clean up when that pane is killed for whatever reason, this would be a more determinisitic way of handling it
I’ll inspect the existing `oc` wrappers and tmux launcher cleanup path read-only, then outline a deterministic cleanup plan without changing files yet.

1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
2: # Launch OpenCode inside tmux with alternate-screen disabled so tmux copy-mode
3: # retains scrollback. Restores the prior setting when OpenCode exits.
4: set -euo pipefail
5: 
6: # Determine current pane/window so local tmux state follows this OpenCode UI.
7: WINDOW=${TMUX_PANE:-}
8: STYLE_TARGET=""
9: SYNC_PID=""
10: 
11: if [ -n "${TMUX:-}" ]; then
12:     STYLE_TARGET="$(tmux display-message -p '#{window_id}' 2>/dev/null || true)"
13:     export TMUX_AGENT_TARGET="$STYLE_TARGET"
14: fi
15: 
16: if [ -z "$STYLE_TARGET" ]; then
17:     STYLE_TARGET="$WINDOW"
18: fi
19: 
20: status_style() {
21:     case "$1" in
22:     busy | running | thinking | streaming | error)
23:         printf '%s\n' '#[fg=#f7768e]'
24:         ;;
25:     idle | active)
26:         printf '%s\n' '#[fg=#e0af68]'
27:         ;;
28:     *)
29:         printf '%s\n' '#[fg=#e0af68]'
30:         ;;
31:     esac
32: }
33: 
34: opencode_status() {
35:     local pane_text=""
36: 
37:     if [ -n "$WINDOW" ]; then
38:         pane_text="$(tmux capture-pane -t "$WINDOW" -S -30 -p 2>/dev/null || true)"
39:         if printf '%s\n' "$pane_text" | grep -q 'esc interrupt'; then
40:             printf '%s\n' busy
41:             return 0
42:         fi
43:         if printf '%s\n' "$pane_text" | grep -q 'ctrl[+]p commands'; then
44:             printf '%s\n' idle
45:             return 0
46:         fi
47:     fi
48: 
49:     printf '%s\n' idle
50: }
51: 
52: set_window_style() {
53:     [ -n "$STYLE_TARGET" ] || return 0
54:     tmux set-window-option -t "$STYLE_TARGET" @wname_style "$(status_style "$1")" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
55: }
56: 
57: clear_pane_style() {
58:     [ -n "$WINDOW" ] || return 0
59:     tmux set-option -p -u -t "$WINDOW" @wname_style >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
60: }
61: 
62: sync_window_style() {
63:     local status=""
64: 
65:     while :; do
66:         status="$(opencode_status)"
67:         # Reassert every poll. Other tmux hooks/reloads can clear @wname_style
68:         # without changing the window status, so change-only updates are brittle.
69:         set_window_style "$status"
70:         sleep 0.5
71:     done
72: }
73: 
74: restore_alternate_screen() {
75:     if [ -n "$WINDOW" ]; then
76:         tmux setw -t "$WINDOW" alternate-screen on >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
77:     else
78:         tmux setw -w alternate-screen on >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
79:     fi
80: }
81: 
82: cleanup() {
83:     if [ -n "$SYNC_PID" ]; then
84:         kill "$SYNC_PID" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
85:     fi
86:     clear_pane_style
87:     if [ -n "$STYLE_TARGET" ]; then
88:         tmux set-window-option -t "$STYLE_TARGET" -u @wname_style >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
89:     fi
90:     restore_alternate_screen
91: }
92: 
93: trap cleanup EXIT
94: 
95: if [ -n "$WINDOW" ]; then
96:     tmux setw -t "$WINDOW" alternate-screen off >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
97: else
98:     tmux setw -w alternate-screen off >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
99: fi
100: 
101: if [ -n "$STYLE_TARGET" ]; then
102:     clear_pane_style
103:     set_window_style "$(opencode_status)"
104:     sync_window_style &
105:     SYNC_PID="$!"
106: fi
107: 
108: status=0
109: OPENCODE_DIR="$PWD" "$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/bin/oc" "$@" || status="$?"
110: exit "$status"

(End of file - total 110 lines)
</content>

metadata.preview:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Launch OpenCode inside tmux with alternate-screen disabled so tmux copy-mode
# retains scrollback. Restores the prior setting when OpenCode exits.
set -euo pipefail

# Determine current pane/window so local tmux state follows this OpenCode UI.
WINDOW=${TMUX_PANE:-}
STYLE_TARGET=""
SYNC_PID=""

if [ -n "${TMUX:-}" ]; then
    STYLE_TARGET="$(tmux display-message -p '#{window_id}' 2>/dev/null || true)"
    export TMUX_AGENT_TARGET="$STYLE_TARGET"
fi

if [ -z "$STYLE_TARGET" ]; then
    STYLE_TARGET="$WINDOW"
fi

state.title: tmux-open|scripts/bin/oc|opencode

parts.text:
Agreed. Deterministic owner-based cleanup is better than trying to infer staleness later.

Current shape:

- `scripts/opencode/tmux-open.sh` runs `scripts/bin/oc` in the pane and has `trap cleanup EXIT`.
- `scripts/bin/oc` ends with `exec opencode attach ...`, so the attach process replaces the wrapper.
- If tmux kills the pane cleanly, the foreground process should usually receive terminal/session signals, but there is no explicit pane-id-to-attach-PID cleanup path.
- That leaves edge cases where an `ocv attach` process can outlive the pane and keep a listener subscription open.

Plan I’d implement after plan mode:

1. Add a pane-owned attach registry.
   - State dir: `${XDG_STATE_HOME:-$HOME/.local/state}/opencode/attaches/`
   - Keyed by tmux pane id, sanitized, e.g. `%42` -> `pane-42.pid`
   - Value includes attach PID, cwd, start time, and command.

2. Change `scripts/opencode/tmux-open.sh` to launch `scripts/bin/oc` as a tracked child.
   - Start child in background.
   - Write child PID against `$TMUX_PANE`.
   - `wait` for it and preserve exit status.
   - Trap `EXIT`, `HUP`, `INT`, `TERM`.
   - On cleanup, kill the registered child process if still alive, remove registry, then restore tmux styles/alternate screen.

3. Add a tmux `pane-exited` / pane cleanup hook.
   - When tmux reports pane exit/kill, call a small cleanup script with `#{pane_id}`.
   - That script reads the registered PID for that pane and kills it if still alive.
   - This handles cases where the wrapper trap doesn’t get a normal chance to run.

4. Add a safe `opencode-service clients` command.
   - Show pane id, PID, age, cwd, and whether the PID still exists.
   - Mark “orphaned registry” separately from “running attach”.
   - No automatic killing except via the pane-exit path.

5. Add tests.
   - `bash -n scripts/opencode/tmux-open.sh`
   - `bash -n scripts/opencode/cleanup-pane.sh` if added.
   - `fish -n .config/fish/functions/opencode-service.fish`
   - Extend `scripts/test-filter.sh opencode` to assert registry cleanup wiring exists.
   - Manual test: open OpenCode in a tmux split, kill the pane, verify no matching `ocv attach ... --dir <that cwd>` remains.

Key design constraint: cleanup should be based on ownership (`tmux pane id -> attach PID`), not age, cwd, or guesswork.
How much overhead would this add?

**Considering compression needs**

I need to think about how to compress all the information from m0001 to m0017 effectively, and maybe I should close them out. There's an active question about overhead, which could depend on the plan I choose. I want to make sure I'm including everything necessary while still being efficient. Compression of the older explorations is definitely a priority for making sure we're staying organized!

content.summary:
User reported OpenCode console warning text: `Possible EventTarget memory leak detected. 11 destroyed listeners added to [P2]. MaxListeners is undefined. Use events.setMaxListeners() to increase limit` and asked whether `serve.sh` for `opencode-server` needs optimisation. Loaded `customize-opencode` skill because task concerns opencode config/server. Investigation found `scripts/opencode/serve.sh` is a very thin wrapper: sets `PORT=${OPENCODE_PORT:-4096}`, ensures `${XDG_STATE_HOME:-$HOME/.local/state}/opencode/server.password`, exports `OPENTUI_GRAPHICS=0`, `OPENCODE_DISABLE_LSP_DOWNLOAD=true`, `OPENCODE_SERVER_USERNAME=opencode`, then `exec "$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/bin/opencode" serve --port "$PORT"`. `Library/LaunchAgents/com.dotfiles.opencode-serve.plist` runs this script with `KeepAlive`, `ThrottleInterval 30`, logs to `~/.local/state/opencode/serve.out.log` and `serve.err.log`, sets PATH, HOME, OPENCODE_PORT=4096, OPENTUI_GRAPHICS=0, OPENCODE_DISABLE_LSP_DOWNLOAD=true, OPENCODE_SERVER_USERNAME=opencode. `scripts/bin/opencode` is a shim that exports `OPENTUI_GRAPHICS=0` and execs an `ocv` binary from `/opt/homebrew/bin/ocv`, `/usr/local/bin/ocv`, `$HOME/.local/bin/ocv`, or `$HOME/.bun/bin/ocv`, otherwise falls back to `ocv` in PATH.

OpenCode config at `.config/opencode/opencode.json` includes plugins `@tarquinen/opencode-dcp@latest`, `./plugin/opencode-with-claude.ts`, `./plugin/claude-compat.ts`, `./plugin/session-env.ts`, `./plugin/nvim-open.ts`, `lsp: true`, `compaction.auto/prune/reserved`, watcher ignore patterns, and instructions `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`. DCP configs: `.opencode/dcp.jsonc` has DCP enabled with `debug:false`, `pruneNotification:"minimal"`, `pruneNotificationType:"chat"`, commands enabled, compress nudgeFrequency 10, deduplication and purgeErrors strategies enabled; `.config/opencode/dcp.jsonc` only has schema. Local plugin scan found no direct `addEventListener`; relevant plugin code handles `server.instance.disposed` events but does not add listeners directly. `claude-compat.ts` has a large event handler and explicitly ignores `server.instance.disposed` to avoid treating transient server disposal as TUI exit. `session-env.ts` also ignores server disposal for metadata. `entire.ts` (auto-generated) treats `server.instance.disposed` as session end and calls `entire hooks opencode session-end` synchronously if `currentSessionID` is set. `sse-recorder.ts` records `server.instance.disposed` among default events and only uses child process `on('error')`/`on('close')` for Entire hook subprocesses. Warning stack in `~/.local/state/opencode/serve.err.log` showed `MaxListenersExceededWarning: Possible EventTarget memory leak detected. 11 event listeners added to [DL]` and another for `[by]`, both type `event`, stack through Bun bundled `~effect/Effect/evaluate` chunks. `serve.out.log` only contained three `opencode server listening on http://127.0.0.1:4096` lines. `launchctl print gui/$(id -u)/com.dotfiles.opencode-serve` showed service running with pid `89765`, runs=2, last terminating signal 15, not crash-looping. `ps` showed one server `/opt/homebrew/bin/ocv serve --port 4096` plus four attach clients: `/opt/homebrew/bin/ocv attach http://127.0.0.1:4096 --dir /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles` (old, 21h47m), `--dir /Users/shahe.islam/work/aws-account-bootstrapping` (21h50m), `--dir /Users/shahe.islam/obsidian` (18h43m), and another `--dir /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles` (2m13s). OpenCode version was `1.15.13-ocv.4.0`. Conclusion given: `serve.sh` itself likely does not need optimisation; warning is in Bun/Effect/OpenCode runtime or plugins/listeners inside running server and may be caused by multiple long-lived `ocv attach` clients stacking server-side subscriptions. DCP is plausible contributor because UI text near warning matched DCP chat/pruning activity, but stack does not point to local plugin files.

User asked: “How are you defining what a stale attach is?” Answer defined a stale attach as an `ocv attach` client still alive but no longer having a useful interactive owner, not simply old. Practical stale criteria: parent terminal/tmux pane gone but `ocv attach` still running; TTY missing or detached so no user can interact; idle long time and cwd/session not visible in active tmux window; multiple attaches for same repo/session where only one pane is in use; process survives after terminal/pane that launched it closes. The four live attaches were described as “suspicious, not proven stale”; two dotfiles attaches most suspicious only because same server and same dir, but legitimate if both are visible panes. Safe stale check proposed: list PIDs/PPIDs/elapsed/TTY/`--dir`, map PID/PPID to `tmux list-panes -a -F`, mark stale only with no matching live pane/TTY or pane command no longer matching attach process; never kill based on age alone.

User then clarified desired behavior: “These should always automatically clean up when that pane is killed for whatever reason, this would be a more deterministic way of handling it,” referring specifically to stale conditions where parent terminal/tmux pane is gone but `ocv attach` still running or its TTY is missing/uninteractive. Existing cleanup path inspected: `scripts/opencode/tmux-open.sh` launches OpenCode in tmux panes, tracks `WINDOW=${TMUX_PANE:-}`, sets window style and alternate-screen off, starts a background `sync_window_style` loop, has `cleanup()` trap on `EXIT` that kills only `SYNC_PID`, clears tmux style, restores alternate-screen, then line 109 runs `OPENCODE_DIR="$PWD" "$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/bin/oc" "$@" || status="$?"; exit "$status"`. Because it runs `scripts/bin/oc` in the foreground and `scripts/bin/oc` ends with `exec opencode attach "${url}" --dir "${OPENCODE_DIR:-$PWD}" "$@"`, the attach replaces the wrapper in that child process. Current tmux bindings in `.tmux.conf` line 308/309 split panes to run `$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/opencode/tmux-open.sh`; test suite has OpenCode tests around lines 1060-1109 asserting server script, LaunchAgent, oc wrappers, tmux launcher behavior, cleanup trap, and explicitly checks tmux launcher does NOT `exec "$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/bin/oc"`.

Plan proposed for deterministic pane-owned cleanup (not yet implemented): add pane-owned attach registry under `${XDG_STATE_HOME:-$HOME/.local/state}/opencode/attaches/`, keyed by sanitized tmux pane id (e.g. `%42` -> `pane-42.pid`), storing attach PID, cwd, start time, command. Change `scripts/opencode/tmux-open.sh` so instead of direct foreground execution it launches `scripts/bin/oc` as a tracked child in background, writes child PID against `$TMUX_PANE`, waits for it and preserves exit status, traps `EXIT`, `HUP`, `INT`, `TERM`, and on cleanup kills registered child process if alive, removes registry, then restores tmux styles/alternate screen. Add a tmux `pane-exited`/pane cleanup hook that calls a small cleanup script with `#{pane_id}`; cleanup script reads registered PID for that pane and kills if still alive, covering cases where wrapper trap does not run normally. Add safe `opencode-service clients` command showing pane id, PID, age, cwd, whether PID still exists, marking orphaned registry separately from running attach and not auto-killing except via pane-exit path. Add tests: `bash -n scripts/opencode/tmux-open.sh`, `bash -n scripts/opencode/cleanup-pane.sh` if added, `fish -n .config/fish/functions/opencode-service.fish`, extend `scripts/test-filter.sh opencode` for registry cleanup wiring, and manual tmux split kill verifying no matching `ocv attach ... --dir <cwd>` remains. Key design constraint stated: cleanup should be based on ownership (`tmux pane id -> attach PID`), not age/cwd/guesswork. Current active user question asks: “How much overhead would this add?” Plan mode is active, so no edits or system changes are allowed.

state.output: Compressed 17 messages into [Compressed conversation section].

parts.text: ▣ DCP | -87.4K removed, +5.2K summary — Compression #1

Very little.

Expected overhead for the deterministic pane-owned cleanup approach:

| Area | Overhead |
|---|---:|
| Runtime CPU | Essentially zero. One child PID is tracked, then `wait` blocks as it already does today. |
| Memory | Negligible. A few shell variables plus one tiny registry file per active tmux pane. |
| Disk | Tens to hundreds of bytes per active attach under `~/.local/state/opencode/attaches/`. |
| tmux | One `pane-exited` hook invocation when a pane exits. No polling needed. |
| Process count | No extra long-lived process if implemented cleanly. The existing sync loop remains unchanged. |
| Startup latency | Maybe a few milliseconds for `mkdir`, writing the registry file, and launching `oc` in background. |

The only meaningful “cost” is implementation complexity, not machine overhead.

The change would add:

1. A small registry write when `tmux-open.sh` starts an attach.
2. A cleanup trap that kills the owned attach PID if the wrapper exits.
3. A tmux `pane-exited` hook as a backstop if the pane disappears before normal shell cleanup runs.
4. Optionally, an `opencode-service clients` diagnostic command.

The main risk is not performance. It is correctness: making sure the cleanup script only kills the attach PID that was registered for that exact pane, and never kills a newer/reused process by accident. That can be handled by recording PID plus command/cwd/start metadata and verifying the process still looks like `ocv attach ...` before killing.

So: overhead is negligible; safety checks and tests matter more than optimisation.

diffs.patch:
Index: .config/fish/functions/opencode-service.fish
===================================================================
--- .config/fish/functions/opencode-service.fish	
+++ .config/fish/functions/opencode-service.fish	
@@ -1,63 +1,111 @@
 function opencode-service --description "Manage the shared OpenCode launchd server"
     set -l label com.dotfiles.opencode-serve
     set -l uid (id -u)
     set -l plist "$HOME/Library/LaunchAgents/$label.plist"
     set -l source_plist "$HOME/dotfiles/Library/LaunchAgents/$label.plist"
     set -l state_dir "$HOME/.local/state/opencode"
     set -l out_log "$state_dir/serve.out.log"
     set -l err_log "$state_dir/serve.err.log"
     set -l password_file "$state_dir/server.password"
+    set -l attach_dir "$state_dir/attaches"
     set -l port (set -q OPENCODE_PORT; and echo $OPENCODE_PORT; or echo 4096)
     set -l url "http://127.0.0.1:$port"
     set -l cmd (string lower -- (test -n "$argv[1]"; and echo $argv[1]; or echo status))
 
     if test -f "$source_plist"
         mkdir -p "$HOME/Library/LaunchAgents"
         string replace -a __HOME__ "$HOME" <$source_plist | string replace -a __DOTFILES_ROOT__ "$HOME/dotfiles" >$plist
     end
 
     switch "$cmd"
         case start
             mkdir -p "$state_dir"
             launchctl bootstrap "gui/$uid" "$plist" 2>/dev/null; or launchctl kickstart -k "gui/$uid/$label"
         case stop
             launchctl bootout "gui/$uid/$label"
         case restart
             launchctl bootout "gui/$uid/$label" 2>/dev/null
             mkdir -p "$state_dir"
             launchctl bootstrap "gui/$uid" "$plist" 2>/dev/null; or launchctl kickstart -k "gui/$uid/$label"
         case status
             if launchctl list | string match -q "*$label*"
                 echo "$label loaded"
             else
                 echo "$label not loaded"
                 return 1
             end
 
             if command -q curl; and test -s "$password_file"
                 set -l password (string collect <$password_file)
                 command curl -fsS -u "opencode:$password" "$url/path" >/dev/null 2>/dev/null
                 if test $status -eq 0
                     echo "$url healthy"
                 else
                     echo "$url not responding" >&2
                     return 1
                 end
             end
         case logs
             echo "stdout: $out_log"
             test -f "$out_log"; and tail -n 40 "$out_log"
             echo "stderr: $err_log"
             test -f "$err_log"; and tail -n 40 "$err_log"
         case password
             if test -s "$password_file"
                 echo "$password_file"
             else
                 echo "No password file yet; run 'opencode-service start'." >&2
                 return 1
             end
+        case clients
+            if not test -d "$attach_dir"
+                echo "No pane-owned OpenCode attaches registered."
+                return 0
+            end
+
+            printf "%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\n" PANE PID STATE AGE CWD
+            for file in "$attach_dir"/*.pid
+                test -f "$file"; or continue
+                set -l pane ""
+                set -l pid ""
+                set -l cwd ""
+                set -l started ""
+
+                while read -l line
+                    set -l parts (string split -m1 = -- "$line")
+                    set -l key $parts[1]
+                    set -l value $parts[2]
+                    switch $key
+                        case pane
+                            set pane "$value"
+                        case pid
+                            set pid "$value"
+                        case cwd
+                            set cwd "$value"
+                        case started
+                            set started "$value"
+                    end
+                end <$file
+
+                set -l state missing
+                if test -n "$pid"; and kill -0 "$pid" >/dev/null 2>/dev/null
+                    set -l command_line (ps -p "$pid" -o command= 2>/dev/null)
+                    if string match -q "*ocv attach*" -- "$command_line"; or string match -q "*opencode attach*" -- "$command_line"; or string match -q "*scripts/bin/oc*" -- "$command_line"
+                        set state running
+                    else
+                        set state pid-reused
+                    end
+                end
+
+                set -l age unknown
+                if test -n "$started"; and string match -qr '^[0-9]+$' -- $started
+                    set age (math (date +%s) - $started)s
+                end
+
+                printf "%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\n" (test -n "$pane"; and echo "$pane"; or basename "$file" .pid) (test -n "$pid"; and echo "$pid"; or echo -) "$state" "$age" "$cwd"
+            end
         case '*'
-            echo "Usage: opencode-service [start|stop|restart|status|logs|password]" >&2
+            echo "Usage: opencode-service [start|stop|restart|status|logs|password|clients]" >&2
             return 2
     end
 end

Index: .tmux.conf
===================================================================
--- .tmux.conf	
+++ .tmux.conf	
@@ -1,389 +1,392 @@
 # Basic tmux configuration
 set -g default-terminal "tmux-256color"
 set -ga terminal-overrides ",*:Tc:RGB,xterm-256color:Ms=\\E]52;c;%p2%s\\7,tmux-256color:Ms=\\E]52;c;%p2%s\\7"
 
 # Set prefix to Ctrl-s (more ergonomic than Ctrl-Space)
 set -g prefix C-s
 unbind C-b
 unbind C-a
 # Unbind C-z to ensure it's passed through to nested applications (like Neovim terminal)
 unbind C-z
 # Double tap Ctrl-s to send literal Ctrl-s to applications
 bind-key C-s send-prefix
 
 # Enable key repeat
 set -g repeat-time 0
 
 # Basic settings
 set -g mouse on
 set -g extended-keys on
 set -g extended-keys-format csi-u
 set -g escape-time 0
 set -g focus-events on
 set -g history-limit 100000
 set -g base-index 1
 setw -g pane-base-index 1
 set -g renumber-windows on
 setw -g automatic-rename off
 
 # Prevent Claude Code nested-session false positives in tmux
 set-environment -gr CLAUDECODE
 
 # When a session is destroyed, switch to the most recently active session
 # instead of detaching the client (which would close the terminal).
 # Tested with tmux 3.5a.
 set -g detach-on-destroy off
 
 # Proactively ensure "main" session exists at server start.
 # Combined with the session-closed hook below, this guarantees a fallback
 # target is always available for detach-on-destroy.
 # exit-empty is set to off so the server survives the brief window between
 # sole-session destruction and hook recreation.
 set -g exit-empty off
 run-shell 'tmux has-session -t main 2>/dev/null || tmux new-session -d -s main'
 
 # Reactively recreate "main" if destroyed while the server is still running.
 set-hook -g session-closed 'if-shell "! tmux has-session -t main 2>/dev/null" "new-session -d -s main"'
 
 # Activity monitoring - custom completion detection
 # Monitors activity, then checks for completion after silence period
 setw -g monitor-activity on
 set -g visual-activity off  # Don't show visual alerts
 set -g activity-action any  # Trigger on ALL windows including current (enables Claude highlighting)
 # Disable visual styling for windows with activity (no grey background)
 set -g window-status-activity-style "fg=#97958e,bg=#262029"
 
 # Disable silence monitoring (alert-silence hook doesn't fire reliably)
 setw -g monitor-silence 0
 set -g visual-silence off
 
 # Agent activity monitoring:
 #   Claude: event-driven via scripts/bin/claude wrapper (injects --settings hooks)
 #   OpenCode: event-driven via tmux-status plugin plus tmux-open state polling
 # Explicitly unset stale hooks from earlier config versions
 set-hook -gu alert-activity
 set-hook -gu after-select-window
 
 # Bell on activity completion - disabled
 set -g bell-action none
 set -g visual-bell off  # Disable visual bell flash
 
 # Clipboard integration
 set -g set-clipboard on
 # Allow OSC-52 escape sequences to pass through to the terminal
 # Required for Neovim clipboard to work in SSH/container sessions
 set -g allow-passthrough on
 
 # OS-specific default shell configuration
 # reattach-to-user-namespace removed: not needed since macOS 12 (Monterey)
 # pbcopy/pbpaste and OSC-52 work natively in tmux on modern macOS
 # NOTE: default-command is set here to block tmux-sensible from overriding it
 # with "reattach-to-user-namespace -l $SHELL" (which spawns zsh, not fish).
 # After TPM loads, default-command is reset to empty (see post-TPM section)
 # so tmux launches default-shell directly without nesting.
 if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \
   "set -g default-shell /opt/homebrew/bin/fish; \
    set -g default-command /opt/homebrew/bin/fish" \
   "set -g default-shell /usr/bin/fish; \
    set -g default-command /usr/bin/fish"
 
 # Enhanced Copy Mode Configuration
 # Use vi-style keys in copy mode
 setw -g mode-keys vi
 
 # Easy copy mode entry with prefix
 # Use v with prefix (Ctrl-s v) instead of [
 bind-key v copy-mode
 # Keep the default [ binding as well
 bind-key [ copy-mode
 
 # Copy mode key bindings (vi-style)
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi v send-keys -X begin-selection
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi C-v send-keys -X rectangle-toggle
 # Copy to the system clipboard and exit copy mode, matching Vim yank expectations.
 # OS-specific clipboard command: pbcopy on macOS, xclip on Linux
 if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Enter send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Enter send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'"
 
 # Clean copy: Y joins lines (fixes copy-paste line breaks from TUI apps like Claude Code)
 # Regular y = raw copy, Y = join lines (for commands), Alt-y = smart (preserve paragraphs)
 # Note: C-y is NOT used here as it conflicts with vi copy-mode scroll-up
 # Note: M-y requires terminal Alt/Meta key support (works in Ghostty/WezTerm/iTerm2).
 #   Y (Shift-y) is the primary binding and works in all terminals.
 # Note: tmux smart mode is a lightweight paragraph-joiner. For full-featured mode
 #   with list/indent/code-fence preservation, use Fish: cfx -p
 if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-copy-cleanup.sh join'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi M-y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-copy-cleanup.sh smart'" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-copy-cleanup.sh join'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi M-y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-copy-cleanup.sh smart'"
 
 # Additional convenient bindings for copy mode
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Space send-keys -X begin-selection
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Escape send-keys -X cancel
 # Match Neovim scroll convention: C-d scrolls up, C-f scrolls down.
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi C-d send-keys -X halfpage-up
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi C-f send-keys -X halfpage-down
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi C-y send-keys -X scroll-up
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi C-e send-keys -X scroll-down
 # Double-click to select word, triple-click to select line
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi DoubleClick1Pane select-pane \; send-keys -X select-word
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi TripleClick1Pane select-pane \; send-keys -X select-line
 
 # Search bindings in copy mode
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi / command-prompt -i -p "search down" "send -X search-forward-incremental \"%%%\""
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi ? command-prompt -i -p "search up" "send -X search-backward-incremental \"%%%\""
 
 # Key bindings
 # NOTE: Context-aware 'r' key binding is defined AFTER TPM loads (see bottom of file)
 # to override plugin reload bindings
 
 # Window management
 bind c new-window -c "#{pane_current_path}"
 bind % split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
 bind '"' split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"
 # Pane navigation handled by vim-tmux-navigator (Ctrl-h/j/k/l)
 # Keeping prefix+h/j/k/l as fallback when vim-tmux-navigator is not active
 # bind h select-pane -L
 # bind j select-pane -D
 # bind k select-pane -U
 # bind l select-pane -R
 
 # Smart adaptive splitting - automatically chooses horizontal or vertical based on pane dimensions
 # Uses native tmux format arithmetic (no shell spawn): 8*width vs 20*height accounts for ~2:1 char aspect ratio
 bind -r Space if-shell -F "#{e|<:#{e|*:8,#{pane_width}},#{e|*:20,#{pane_height}}}" \
   "splitw -v -c '#{pane_current_path}' ; display '#[fg=#d3daad]↔ Split Horizontal#[default]'" \
   "splitw -h -c '#{pane_current_path}' ; display '#[fg=#a7e1e8]↕ Split Vertical#[default]'"
 
 # Respawn pane keybinding (restart dead/frozen process)
 bind R respawn-pane -k \; display '#[fg=#d3daad] Pane Respawned#[default]'
 
 # M and C-m bindings removed — watcher colors are purely realtime, no manual clear needed
 
 # Toggle logging - save pane output to a file (press again to stop)
 bind L pipe-pane -o "cat >> ~/dotfiles/tmux-logs/\$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M)-#W.log" \; \
   display '#[fg=#efcfab]📝 Logging: ~/dotfiles/tmux-logs/\$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M)-#W.log#[default]'
 
 # List of plugins
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tpm'
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-sensible'
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-yank'
 # set -g @plugin 'alexwforsythe/tmux-which-key'  # Disabled - conflicts with Space key binding
 set -g @plugin 'sainnhe/tmux-fzf'
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-open'
 # Configure tmux-open
 set -g @open-S 'https://www.google.com/search?q='
 # Use 'o' to open URLs/files in copy mode
 set -g @open 'o'
 # Use Ctrl-o to open in editor
 set -g @open-editor 'C-o'
 # tmux-copycat removed: deprecated, slow regex on scrollback. Use native / search in copy mode
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-pain-control'
 # vim-tmux-navigator: seamless navigation between vim and tmux
 set -g @plugin 'christoomey/vim-tmux-navigator'
 # tmux-thumbs - fast Rust-based text hints (replacement for tmux-fingers)
 set -g @plugin 'fcsonline/tmux-thumbs'
 set -g @thumbs-key F
 # OS-specific clipboard command for tmux-thumbs
 if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \
   "set -g @thumbs-command 'echo -n {} | pbcopy'" \
   "set -g @thumbs-command 'echo -n {} | xclip -selection clipboard'"
 set -g @thumbs-upcase-command 'open {}'  # Shift+hint opens URL/path
 set -g @thumbs-hint-fg-color yellow
 set -g @thumbs-hint-bg-color black
 set -g @thumbs-select-fg-color yellow
 set -g @thumbs-select-bg-color black
 
 # tmux-fzf configuration - unified FZF menu for tmux operations
 # Access with: Ctrl-s + f (Find/FZF menu)
 TMUX_FZF_LAUNCH_KEY="f"
 TMUX_FZF_OPTIONS="-p -w 80% -h 80% -m"
 TMUX_FZF_ORDER="session|window|pane|command|keybinding|clipboard"
 
 # tmux session persistence across server/device restarts
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-resurrect'
 set -g @resurrect-processes 'nvim vim "~ocv->ocv"'
 set -g @resurrect-strategy-nvim 'session'
 set -g @resurrect-strategy-vim 'session'
 set -g @resurrect-capture-pane-contents 'on'
 # Claude Code is intentionally excluded here.
 # OpenCode is restored through the local ocv wrapper.
 
 # CPU/battery status plugins removed - loaded but never displayed in native status.
 # tmux-smooth-scroll removed: every scroll tick spawns bash→bash→perl chain
 # Native tmux scrolling is instant and sufficient
 
 # Keep tmux-continuum last: it hooks into status-right for autosave.
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-continuum'
 set -g @continuum-restore 'on'
 # Disable continuum's status-right poller; dotfiles starts a quiet autosave loop
 # after TPM loads so the status bar never executes shell commands while drawing.
 set -g @continuum-save-interval '0'
 set -g @dotfiles-continuum-save-interval '15'
 
 # Miniautumn theme with omerxx-inspired aesthetics
 
 # Session Management - Single unified manager with tmuxinator integration
 # Access with: Ctrl-s + S
 bind S display-popup -E -h 80% -w 80% "$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-session-manager.sh"
 
 # Session Trash Recovery - Browse and restore killed sessions
 # Access with: Ctrl-s + T
 bind T display-popup -E -h 80% -w 80% "$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-session-trash.sh restore"
 
 # Which-key configuration (disabled - conflicts with Space binding)
 # set -g @which-key-popup 1
 
 # =============================================================================
 # STATUS BAR CONFIGURATION - Native Three Section Layout
 # =============================================================================
 # Session (left) | Windows (center) | datetime (right)
 # Window names read @wname_style so agent active/idle colors render directly.
 # =============================================================================
 
 # Status bar layout configuration
 set -g status-position bottom
 set -g status-justify centre              # Center window list
 set -g status-left-length 30              # Space for session indicator
 set -g status-right-length 20             # Space for datetime
 set -g status-interval 15                 # datetime refresh cadence
 
 # Calm transparent status bar styling. Segment backgrounds use default so
 # terminal opacity/blur can show through; the current tab keeps a soft highlight.
 set -g status-style "bg=default,fg=#d7d5cd"
 set -g status-left "#[fg=#97958e,bg=default] #S #[default]"
 set -g status-right '#[fg=#d7d5cd,bg=default] %H:%M '
 
 # Ensure pane backgrounds are not forced to a solid color
 # (lets terminal/wezterm transparency show through and enables nvim winblend)
 set -g window-style 'bg=default'
 set -g window-active-style 'bg=default'
 set -g popup-style 'bg=default,fg=default'
 
 # Window status styling. @wname_style is window-scoped and set by agent launchers.
 setw -g window-status-current-style "fg=#f3f1e9,bg=#332d36,bold"
 setw -g window-status-style "fg=#6f6a73,bg=default"
 setw -g window-status-format '#[range=window|#{window_id}] #{window_index} #[fg=#5e5762]· #{?#{@wname_style},#{@wname_style},}#W '
 setw -g window-status-current-format '#[range=window|#{window_id}] #{window_index} #[fg=#5e5762]· #{?#{@wname_style},#{@wname_style},}#W#{?pane_synchronized, [sync],} '
 
 # Pane borders - subtle with accent on active
 set -g pane-border-style "fg=#423b45"
 set -g pane-active-border-style "fg=#e4caf1"
 
 # Message style
 set -g message-style "bg=#1a141d,fg=#d7d5cd"
 set -g message-command-style "bg=#1a141d,fg=#d7d5cd"
 
 # Copy mode colors
 set -g mode-style "bg=#423b45,fg=#f3f1e9"
 
 # # Update status bar every 30 seconds for CPU/RAM (OPTIMIZED: reduced from 10s)
 # # This reduces refresh overhead by 3x while still providing timely updates
 # set -g status-interval 30
 
 # Auto-install TPM if not present
 if "test ! -d ~/.tmux/plugins/tpm" \
    "run 'git clone https://github.com/tmux-plugins/tpm ~/.tmux/plugins/tpm && ~/.tmux/plugins/tpm/bin/install_plugins'"
 
 # Automatically split new windows and run claude (DISABLED)
 # This creates a vertical split with claude in the right pane for every new window
 # set-hook -g after-new-window 'split-window -h -p 40 "claude"'
 
 # Manual keybinding to open Claude Code when needed
 # Prefix + C opens Claude in a vertical split (40% width on the right)
 bind C split-window -h -p 40 "claude --dangerously-skip-permissions"
 
 # Quick launch: Ctrl-s a opens Claude with smart adaptive split
 bind a if-shell -F "#{e|<:#{e|*:8,#{pane_width}},#{e|*:20,#{pane_height}}}" \
   "splitw -v -c '#{pane_current_path}' 'claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --effort max --remote-control' ; display '#[fg=#d3daad]↔ Split Horizontal (Claude)#[default]'" \
   "splitw -h -c '#{pane_current_path}' 'claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --effort max --remote-control' ; display '#[fg=#a7e1e8]↕ Split Vertical (Claude)#[default]'"
 
 # Quick launch: Ctrl-s O opens OpenCode in its allow-permissions setup
 bind O if-shell -F "#{e|<:#{e|*:8,#{pane_width}},#{e|*:20,#{pane_height}}}" \
   "splitw -v -c '#{pane_current_path}' '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/opencode/tmux-open.sh' ; display '#[fg=#d3daad]↔ Split Horizontal (OpenCode)#[default]'" \
   "splitw -h -c '#{pane_current_path}' '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/opencode/tmux-open.sh' ; display '#[fg=#a7e1e8]↕ Split Vertical (OpenCode)#[default]'"
 
+# Ensure pane-owned OpenCode attach clients die with the tmux pane that owns them.
+set-hook -g pane-exited[90] 'run-shell -b "$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/opencode/cleanup-pane.sh \"#{pane_id}\""'
+
 # Quick launch: Ctrl-s e opens Neovim in current directory
 # C-c first to normalize Fish vi-mode state (lands us in insert mode at empty
 # prompt) so the typed `nvim .` isn't eaten as vi-mode normal commands.
 bind e send-keys C-c "nvim ." Enter
 
 # Quick launch: Ctrl-s E opens per-directory gwtt-prompt.local.md with smart adaptive split
 bind E if-shell -F "#{e|<:#{e|*:8,#{pane_width}},#{e|*:20,#{pane_height}}}" \
   "splitw -v -c '#{pane_current_path}' '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/gwtt-prompt-edit.sh' ; display '#[fg=#d3daad]↔ Split Horizontal (gwtt-prompt)#[default]'" \
   "splitw -h -c '#{pane_current_path}' '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/gwtt-prompt-edit.sh' ; display '#[fg=#a7e1e8]↕ Split Vertical (gwtt-prompt)#[default]'"
 
 # URL handler binding (smart: tries visible pane first, falls back to full scrollback)
 bind-key o run-shell -b "$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-url-handler.sh"
 
 # Keep default tmux bindings for navigation
 # Prefix + O: select previous pane
 # Prefix + p: previous window (default tmux behavior)
 
 # Initialize TMUX plugin manager (keep this line at the very bottom of tmux.conf)
 run '~/.tmux/plugins/tpm/tpm'
 
 # Force tmux-open bindings after all plugins load
 run-shell '~/.tmux/plugins/tmux-open/open.tmux'
 
 # Restore prefix+x to kill-pane after plugin bindings load.
 # Enhanced: capture window info before killing for worktree auto-cleanup.
 # Run the wrapper in tmux background so pane close stays responsive.
 bind-key x run-shell -b '~/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-kill-pane-cleanup.sh "#{session_name}" "#{window_name}" "#{window_panes}"'
 
 # Kill window: prefix + X (Shift-x) - closes entire window with worktree cleanup
 bind-key X confirm-before -p 'Kill window "#{window_name}"? (y/n)' \
   "run-shell -b '~/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-worktree-cleanup.sh \"#{session_name}\" \"#{window_name}\"'; kill-window"
 
 # Force status bar position and layout (override plugin defaults)
 set -g status-position bottom
 set -g status-justify centre
 
 # Final overrides to keep transparent panes/popups after plugins load
 set -g window-style 'bg=default'
 set -g window-active-style 'bg=default'
 set -g popup-style 'bg=default,fg=default'
 set -g popup-border-style 'bg=default,fg=default'
 
 # Keep native status refresh interval after TPM plugins load.
 set -g status-interval 15
 
 # Reassert the final status after TPM plugins load and start tmux persistence
 # from a quiet background loop instead of continuum's status-right command.
 run-shell -b '~/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-status-finalize.sh'
 
 # Fix nested fish: reset default-command to empty after TPM loads.
 # tmux-sensible (loaded by TPM) overrides empty default-command with
 # "reattach-to-user-namespace -l $SHELL". By setting it to fish BEFORE TPM,
 # sensible sees non-empty value and leaves it alone. Now we reset to empty
 # so tmux launches default-shell directly (no "fish -c fish" nesting).
 set -g default-command ''
 
 # Reassert clipboard copy-mode bindings after TPM plugins load.
 # tmux-yank can override these with legacy reattach-to-user-namespace commands.
 if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Enter send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Enter send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'"
 
 # Colored choose-tree session/window picker (must be after TPM)
 # Tree labels (session/window names, markers) use pane fg — set via select-pane -P
 # pane-mode-changed hook resets pane style when exiting choose-tree
 # Agent colors use @wname_style: working=red, idle=yellow, exited=default.
 set-hook -g pane-mode-changed[100] 'if -F "#{pane_in_mode}" "" "select-pane -P default"'
 bind s select-pane -P 'fg=#d3daad' \; choose-tree -Zw -F '#{?pane_format,#[fg=#a7e1e8]#{pane_current_command} #[fg=#97958e](#{pane_current_path}),#{?window_format,#{@wname_style}#{window_name}#{?window_active, *,},#[fg=#97958e]#{session_windows} windows#{?session_attached, #[fg=#efcfab](attached),}}}'
 bind w select-pane -P 'fg=#d3daad' \; choose-tree -Zw -F '#{?pane_format,#[fg=#a7e1e8]#{pane_current_command} #[fg=#97958e](#{pane_current_path}),#{?window_format,#{@wname_style}#{window_name}#{?window_active, *,},#[fg=#97958e]#{session_windows} windows#{?session_attached, #[fg=#efcfab](attached),}}}'
 
 # Context-aware r key: recall when in Claude, config reload otherwise
 # Must be after TPM to override plugin reload bindings
 bind r if-shell "ps -o args= -t #{pane_tty} 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'claude'" \
     "confirm-before -p 'Kill Claude pane and run recall? (y/n)' 'respawn-pane -k \"/opt/homebrew/bin/recall\"'" \
     "source-file ~/.tmux.conf \\; display '#[fg=#d3daad]󰑓 Config Reloaded#[default]'"

Index: scripts/opencode/cleanup-pane.sh
===================================================================
--- scripts/opencode/cleanup-pane.sh	
+++ scripts/opencode/cleanup-pane.sh	
@@ -0,0 +1,48 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -euo pipefail
+
+pane_id="${1:-}"
+state_home="${XDG_STATE_HOME:-$HOME/.local/state}"
+attach_dir="$state_home/opencode/attaches"
+
+if [ -z "$pane_id" ]; then
+	exit 0
+fi
+
+pane_key() {
+	local pane="$1"
+	pane="${pane#%}"
+	printf 'pane-%s' "$(printf '%s' "$pane" | tr -c '[:alnum:]_.-' '_')"
+}
+
+attach_file="$attach_dir/$(pane_key "$pane_id").pid"
+
+if [ ! -f "$attach_file" ]; then
+	exit 0
+fi
+
+pid=""
+while IFS='=' read -r key value; do
+	case "$key" in
+	pid)
+		pid="$value"
+		;;
+	esac
+done <"$attach_file"
+
+rm -f "$attach_file" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
+
+if [ -z "$pid" ] || ! printf '%s' "$pid" | grep -Eq '^[0-9]+$'; then
+	exit 0
+fi
+
+if ! kill -0 "$pid" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+	exit 0
+fi
+
+command_line="$(ps -p "$pid" -o command= 2>/dev/null || true)"
+case "$command_line" in
+*"opencode attach"* | *"ocv attach"* | *"scripts/bin/oc"*)
+	kill "$pid" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
+	;;
+esac

+
+	mkdir -p "$ATTACH_DIR"
+	ATTACH_FILE="$ATTACH_DIR/$(pane_key "$WINDOW").pid"
+	{
+		printf 'pid=%s\n' "$ATTACH_PID"
+		printf 'pane=%s\n' "$WINDOW"
+		printf 'cwd=%s\n' "$PWD"
+		printf 'started=%s\n' "$(date +%s)"
+		printf 'command=%s\n' "$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/bin/oc $*"
+	} >"$ATTACH_FILE"
+}
+
+unregister_attach() {
+	[ -n "$ATTACH_FILE" ] || return 0
+	rm -f "$ATTACH_FILE" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
+}
+
+kill_attach() {
+	[ -n "$ATTACH_PID" ] || return 0
+	kill -0 "$ATTACH_PID" >/dev/null 2>&1 || return 0
+	kill "$ATTACH_PID" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
+}
+
 cleanup() {
-    if [ -n "$SYNC_PID" ]; then
-        kill "$SYNC_PID" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
-    fi
-    clear_pane_style
-    if [ -n "$STYLE_TARGET" ]; then
-        tmux set-window-option -t "$STYLE_TARGET" -u @wname_style >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
-    fi
-    restore_alternate_screen
+	kill_attach
+	unregister_attach
+	if [ -n "$SYNC_PID" ]; then
+		kill "$SYNC_PID" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
+	fi
+	clear_pane_style
+	if [ -n "$STYLE_TARGET" ]; then
+		tmux set-window-option -t "$STYLE_TARGET" -u @wname_style >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
+	fi
+	restore_alternate_screen
 }
 
 trap cleanup EXIT
+trap 'status=129; cleanup; trap - EXIT; exit "$status"' HUP
+trap 'status=130; cleanup; trap - EXIT; exit "$status"' INT
+trap 'status=143; cleanup; trap - EXIT; exit "$status"' TERM
 
 if [ -n "$WINDOW" ]; then
-    tmux setw -t "$WINDOW" alternate-screen off >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
+	tmux setw -t "$WINDOW" alternate-screen off >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
 else
-    tmux setw -w alternate-screen off >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
+	tmux setw -w alternate-screen off >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
 fi
 
 if [ -n "$STYLE_TARGET" ]; then
-    clear_pane_style
-    set_window_style "$(opencode_status)"
-    sync_window_style &
-    SYNC_PID="$!"
+	clear_pane_style
+	set_window_style "$(opencode_status)"
+	sync_window_style &
+	SYNC_PID="$!"
 fi
 
 status=0
-OPENCODE_DIR="$PWD" "$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/bin/oc" "$@" || status="$?"
+OPENCODE_DIR="$PWD" "$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/bin/oc" "$@" &
+ATTACH_PID="$!"
+register_attach "$@"
+wait "$ATTACH_PID" || status="$?"
 exit "$status"

Index: scripts/test-filter.sh
